title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Tea has (0) a significant role in cultures around the world for thousands of years. According to legend, the drink was first discovered in China when tea leaves accidentally (1) into boiling water. From China, tea (2) to Japan, where it became central to religious ceremonies. European traders (3) across tea in the 16th century and quickly recognised its commercial potential. The British, in particular, (4) to the beverage with great enthusiasm, and it soon became their national drink. Tea plantations were (5) up in India and Sri Lanka to meet the growing demand. The tradition of afternoon tea (6) on among the upper classes in the 19th century and eventually became popular with everyone. Today, tea remains the second most consumed drink in the world after water. Whether enjoyed with milk and sugar or served plain, tea continues to (7) people together in homes and cafés everywhere. Its rich history and cultural significance (8) for its enduring popularity across generations.

example.explanation: 'Played a role' is the fixed expression to indicate that something is important.

questions.explanation: 'Fell into' is the most natural verb to indicate that something falls accidentally.
'Spread to' indicates that something spread to other places.
'Came across' (with 'across' already in the text) means to find something by chance.
'Took to' (with 'to' already in the text) means to start liking or becoming fond of something.
'Set up' (with 'up' already in the text) means to establish or create something new.
'Caught on' (with 'on' already in the text) means to become popular or fashionable.
'Bring people together' significa reunir o unir a las personas.
'Account for' (with 'for' already in the text) means to explain or be the reason for something.
